Disease_Risk.description: Indicates presence of any retinal abnormality

DR.description: Damage to retina due to diabetes

ARMD.description: Degeneration of macula causing vision loss

MH.description: A small break in the macula affecting central vision

DN.description: Kidney damage due to diabetes, often with retinal manifestations

BRVO.description: Blockage of small veins draining blood from the retina

TSLN.description: Visible choroidal vessels due to thin retinal pigment epithelium

ERM.description: Thin scar tissue forming on the macula surface

LS.description: Thinning of the peripheral retina predisposing to tears

MS.description: Optic neuritis or retinal nerve fiber layer changes

CSR.description: Fluid accumulation under the retina causing detachment

ODC.description: Enlargement of optic cup, often seen in glaucoma

CRVO.description: Blockage of the main retinal vein causing widespread hemorrhage

TV.description: Abnormally twisted or curved retinal blood vessels

AH.description: Vision distortion due to irregular eye shape

ODP.description: Calcified deposits in the optic nerve head

ODE.description: Swelling of the optic disc due to increased intracranial pressure

AION.description: Loss of blood flow to the optic nerve head

PT.description: Separation of vitreous gel from the retina

CRS.description: Break in the choroid due to trauma

EDN.description: Corneal or retinal vascular endothelial abnormality

CWS.description: Fluffy white spots from nerve fiber layer ischemia

ODPM.description: Macular detachment associated with optic disc pit

MNF.description: White patches of myelinated retinal nerve fibers

HR.description: Retinal damage due to high blood pressure

CRAO.description: Blockage of the main retinal artery causing sudden vision loss

PTCR.description: Break in the lens posterior capsule

VS.description: Slow blood flow in retinal veins

BRAO.description: Blockage of a branch retinal artery

HPED.description: Bleeding under the retinal pigment epithelium

CL.description: Alternative blood vessel pathways after occlusion
